title: sida quaternata
intro: Sida quaternata, also known as the "Indian cord plant" or " country mallow," is a plant species belonging to the mallow family (Malvaceae). The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, making it beneficial for treating various health conditions. Sida quaternata has been reported to have several health benefits, such as reducing fever, treating skin conditions, and alleviating respiratory issues, among others. The herbal preparations of Sida quaternata include infusion, decoction, and tincture, which can be used to make teas, poultices, and other remedies for various health problems.
